methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting samples from stratigraphic sections and preparing them for different types of paleontological analysis, including the creation of thin sections/polished slabs and the disaggregation and sieving of samples for loose specimens. It also details the repositories where the collected material is housed.

steps.description: This work is based on 11 stratigraphic sections (Fig. 1) systematically and evenly sampled from the base to the top for biostratigraphic purposes. This sampling resulted in the description of 359 samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect geological samples from stratigraphic sections systematically for biostratigraphic analysis.
inputs: Sampling strategy (systematic, even, base to top)

steps.description: The samples were studied in thin sections and polished slabs, except those collected to extract loose, separate specimens of nummulitids and alveolinids.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and study geological samples using thin sections and polished slabs.
inputs: Collected samples (subset not designated for loose specimens)

steps.description: These samples [collected to extract loose, separate specimens of nummulitids and alveolinids] were disaggregated in a water, oxygen peroxide and Na 2 CO 3 solution
steps.searchable_summary: Disaggregate specific geological samples using a chemical solution to extract loose specimens.
inputs: Specific collected samples (for loose nummulitids and alveolinids)

steps.description: and then sieved through mesh apertures of 1.0, 0.5 and 0.2mm.
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve disaggregated geological material through specified mesh sizes.

steps.description: The material studied is housed in three different repositories (Appendix I). The samples from the Isuela and Gabardiella sections (also named Arguis and Lúsera) are housed in the repository of the Instituto Geológico Minero de España (IGME). Some samples are included in the Serra-Kiel collection, provisionally housed in the Department of Earth and Ocean Dynamics, University of Barcelona (UB), and will be definitively deposited in the Museu de Ciències Naturals de Barcelona. Finally, the rest of the samples, including the new species (MPZ 2019/1681-1683and MPZ 2020, are housed in the Natural Science Museum of the University of Zaragoza (Canudo, 2018).
steps.searchable_summary: Store collected and processed geological samples and material in designated repositories.
outputs: Material stored in repositories (IGME, UB/Museu de Ciències Naturals de Barcelona, Natural Science Museum of the University of Zaragoza)
